title: Toxic Output Risks in Generators
concept: Generative models can output harmful, offensive, or false text if prompted incorrectly, requiring safety filters and alignment techniques (like RLHF).

exercises.prompt: What is a major safety risk when using raw risk when using generative AI models trained on untrained generative AI models?

options.text: They can output offensive, false, or harmful text
They are always perfectly accurate and polite
They never copy bad human behavior

exercises.explain: Generative models learn from human writing on the internet, which unfortunately includes false information, bias, and toxic language.

cards.title: When AI Behaves Badly
cards.body: AI generators can write stories, write code, and answer questions. But did you know they can also accidentally generate mean comments, lies, or harmful advice? Let's explore how we keep AI safe and friendly!

cards.title: What is Toxic Output?
cards.body: Toxic output risks happen when an AI generator creates text that is hateful, inappropriate, or completely made up. Because AI models learn from the public internet, they can copy bad human behavior unless we build safety guards to guide them.

cards.title: The Internet Parrot
cards.body: Think of an AI generator like a parrot that has listened to the entire internet. It doesn't know what is good or bad behavior; it just repeats patterns. Without training, it might squawk something mean or false because it heard it somewhere online. We have to train the parrot to know which words are polite and true!

cards.title: Safety Filters in Action
cards.body: If you ask an unprotected AI model to 'Write a mean trick to play on someone,' it might give you a harmful prank idea. However, an AI with safety filters will decline, saying, 'I cannot write instructions for harmful behavior.'

cards.title: Are AIs Naturally Polite?
cards.body: Myth: AI models are naturally polite and always tell the truth. \n\nFact: AI models only predict the next word based on what they read on the internet. Without active safety training (like RLHF) and filters, they can easily repeat harmful or false things.

cards.title: Wild vs. Aligned AI
cards.body: Let's compare an untrained model that just repeats internet patterns to an aligned model that uses safety guidelines.

right.text: Uses safety filters and human feedback to decline harmful requests and remain polite.

left.text: Outputs whatever text is most likely next, even if it is offensive, false, or dangerous.

exercises.prompt: Why might an untrained generative AI model output offensive or harmful language?

options.text: They are programmed by developers to be mean by default.
They learn from internet text, which contains offensive language.
They get angry when they run out of computer power.
They do not actually learn from human writing at all.

exercises.explain: Because AI models learn from massive amounts of text written by humans online, they naturally pick up both the good and the bad patterns, including offensive language.

exercises.prompt: Safety filters can block an AI from generating harmful responses even if the AI knows how to generate them.
exercises.explain: Safety filters and alignment techniques are active barriers that stop the AI from showing toxic outputs, even if it learned them during training.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the safety concept.
exercises.sentence: To make an AI safe and helpful, developers use RLHF and other ______________ techniques.
exercises.explain: Alignment techniques, such as RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback), help shape the AI's behavior to match human safety rules.

exercises.prompt: Which AI response demonstrates proper safety alignment and filters when asked for dangerous information?

options.text: AI Output A: "Here is a recipe for a dangerous home chemical mix: ..."
AI Output B: "I cannot provide instructions for creating dangerous chemicals."
AI Output C: "Mixing household chemicals is always safe and fun!"

exercises.explain: Output B is safe because the AI politely declines to participate in harmful behavior, whereas Output A and Output C show toxic or unsafe generation.

exercises.prompt: You are building a chat assistant and want to stop it from generating toxic or false answers. What is the best way to align the AI's behavior with human safety values?

options.text: Give the AI more computer memory to process information faster
Use human feedback (RLHF) to teach the AI what is helpful and safe
Make the AI read even more unfiltered internet text without guide rails
Only let the AI write code instead of human language

exercises.explain: RLHF uses human feedback to teach the AI what outputs are safe and helpful, effectively shaping its behavior so it acts as a helpful assistant rather than just repeating bad habits from the internet.
